http::
PREFIX xsd:
SELECT ?functionName ?openAddr ?allocAddr ?writeAddr WHERE { # Get the function object and extract its name from the URI ?func a ontology:Function ; ontology:containsInstruction ?inst1, ?inst2, ?inst3 .
# Extracts the part after the '#' or '/' in the URI BIND(REPLACE(STR(?func), "^.*[#/]", "") AS ?functionName)
# 1. OpenProcess ?inst1 ontology:hasOpcode "CALL" ; ontology:hasSourceOperand ?api1 ; ontology:hasDestinationOperand ?handleReg ; # Tracking the returned handle ontology:atAddress ?addr1 . FILTER(CONTAINS(STR(?api1), "OpenProcess"))
# 2. VirtualAllocEx (occurring after OpenProcess) ?inst2 ontology:hasOpcode "CALL" ; ontology:hasSourceOperand ?api2 ; ontology:hasSourceOperand ?handleReg ; # Dataflow check ontology:atAddress ?addr2 . FILTER(CONTAINS(STR(?api2), "VirtualAllocEx")) FILTER(xsd:integer(?addr2) > xsd:integer(?addr1))
# 3. WriteProcessMemory (occurring after VirtualAllocEx) ?inst3 ontology:hasOpcode "CALL" ; ontology:hasSourceOperand ?api3 ; ontology:hasSourceOperand ?handleReg ; # Dataflow check ontology:atAddress ?addr3 . FILTER(CONTAINS(STR(?api3), "WriteProcessMemory")) FILTER(xsd:integer(?addr3) > xsd:integer(?addr2))
# Optional: Tracking the allocated memory address (RDX/RAX depending on convention) ?inst2 ontology:hasDestinationOperand ?memAddrReg . ?inst3 ontology:hasSourceOperand ?memAddrReg . }
SELECT ?function ?instr1 ?addr1 ?instr2 ?addr2 ?operandValue WHERE { # Find an instruction that targets a persistence-related API ?instr1 ontology:hasDestinationOperand ?apiName ; ontology:atAddress ?addr1 .
# Filter for relevant API calls FILTER(REGEX(STR(?apiName), "RegCreateKey|RegSetValue|CreateService|CopyFile", "i"))
# Find a nearby instruction (likely preceding) that loads the target path ?instr2 ontology:hasSourceOperand ?operandValue ; ontology:atAddress ?addr2 .
# Heuristic: The path is usually loaded within a few bytes/instructions of the call FILTER(?addr2 < ?addr1 && ?addr2 > (?addr1 - 32))
# Filter for suspicious indicators in the operand FILTER(REGEX(STR(?operandValue), "Run|RunOnce|Start Menu|Startup", "i")) } ORDER BY ?addr1
SELECT ?function ?instruction ?opcode ?targetAPI ?address WHERE { # Locate the instruction and its address ?instruction ontology:atAddress ?addrObj ; ontology:opcode "call" . ?addrObj ontology:value ?address .
# Identify if the call target is a known networking API ?instruction ontology:hasDestinationOperand ?targetAPI . FILTER(?targetAPI IN ("socket", "connect", "send", "recv", "WinHttpSendRequest", "InternetOpenUrl"))
# Link back to the containing function if your ontology allows ?function ontology:containsInstruction ?instruction . } ORDER BY ?address
SELECT ?allocFunc ?jumpInst ?retParam WHERE { # 1. Identify the VirtualAlloc function by its string name ?allocFunc a ontology:Function . FILTER(CONTAINS(STR(?allocFunc), "VirtualAlloc"))
# 2. Find the parameter defining PAGE_EXECUTE_READWRITE (0x40) ?permParam ontology:passesInto ?allocFunc . # Filtering by the URI or Label of the parameter for the hex value FILTER(CONTAINS(STR(?permParam), "0x40") || CONTAINS(STR(?permParam), "PAGE_EXECUTE_READWRITE"))
# 3. Identify the parameter variable returned by the allocation ?allocFunc ontology:returns ?retParam .
# 4. Find an indirect jump instruction ?program ontology:containsInstruction ?jumpInst . ?jumpInst ontology:hasOpcode "jmp" ; ontology:hasSourceOperand ?retParam ; # Tracking the returned variable to the jump ontology:atAddress ?jumpAddr .
# 5. Logical Sequence: Ensure the jump's address is higher than the allocation function ?allocFunc ontology:atAddress ?allocAddr . FILTER(?jumpAddr > ?allocAddr) }
SELECT (COUNT (*) as ?total) WHERE { ?instance a ontology:DLL }
SELECT (COUNT (*) as ?total) WHERE { ?instance a ontology:Instruction }
SELECT ?function (COUNT(DISTINCT ?opcode) AS ?uniqueOpcodes) (COUNT(?instruction) AS ?totalInstructions) ((COUNT(DISTINCT ?opcode) * 1.0 / COUNT(?instruction)) AS ?diversityRatio)
WHERE { ?function ontology:containsInstruction ?instruction . ?instruction ontology:hasOpcode ?opcode . HAVING (?totalInstructions > 10). } GROUP BY ?function ORDER BY DESC(?diversityRatio)
SELECT ?function (COUNT(?inst) AS ?totalRelevantInst) WHERE { ?function ontology:containsInstruction ?inst . ?inst ontology:hasOpcode ?opcode .
BIND(LCASE(STR(?opcode)) AS ?opStr)
# 1. Ensure the function has at least one TRANSFORMATION opcode FILTER EXISTS { ?function ontology:containsInstruction ?i_trans . ?i_trans ontology:hasOpcode ?op_trans . FILTER(?op_trans IN ("xor", "add", "sub", "ror", "rol", "not")) }
# 2. Ensure the same function has at least one LOOP/BRANCH opcode FILTER EXISTS { ?function ontology:containsInstruction ?i_loop . ?i_loop ontology:hasOpcode ?op_loop . FILTER(?op_loop IN ("jnz", "jz", "jmp", "loop")) }
# Only count the instructions that match our overall 'suspicious' list FILTER(?opcode IN ("xor", "add", "sub", "ror", "rol", "not", "jnz", "jz", "jmp", "loop")) } GROUP BY ?function HAVING (COUNT(?inst) >= 2) ORDER BY DESC(?totalRelevantInst)
SELECT DISTINCT ?function WHERE { # 1. Find a function that contains an Anti-VM/Debugger indicator ?function ontology:containsInstruction ?apiCall . ?apiCall ontology:hasOpcode "CALL" ; ontology:hasOperand ?apiName .
FILTER(?apiName IN ("IsDebuggerPresent", "CheckRemoteDebuggerPresent", "NtQueryInformationProcess"))
# 2. Ensure the SAME function also contains a conditional jump ?function ontology:containsInstruction ?jumpInstr . ?jumpInstr ontology:hasOpcode ?jumpOpcode .
# Filter for common x86 conditional jump opcodes (JZ, JNZ, JE, JNE, etc.) FILTER(REGEX(STR(?jumpOpcode), "^J[A-Z]{1,2}$", "i")) }
SELECT ?function ?instrName1 ?instrName2 WHERE { # Bind the same function to multiple instructions ?function ontology:containsInstruction ?instr1 ; ontology:containsInstruction ?instr2 ; ontology:containsInstruction ?instr3 .
# Identify the call to OpenProcessToken ?instr1 ontology:hasOpcode "CALL" ; ontology:hasSourceOperand "OpenProcessToken" .
# Identify the loading of the privilege string (source or destination) ?instr2 ontology:hasSourceOperand ?val . FILTER(CONTAINS(LCASE(STR(?val)), "sedebugprivilege"))
# Identify the call to AdjustTokenPrivileges ?instr3 ontology:hasOpcode "CALL" ; ontology:hasSourceOperand "AdjustTokenPrivileges" .
# Ensure we aren't matching the exact same instruction object FILTER(?instr1 != ?instr2 && ?instr2 != ?instr3) }
SELECT DISTINCT ?functionURI ?functionName ?suspiciousPath ?instructionAddress WHERE { # 1. Identify the function and extract its name from the URI ?func a ontology:Function . BIND(STR(?func) AS ?funcString) # Extract the part after the last '#' or '/' to get the "name" BIND(REPLACE(?funcString, "^.*[#/]", "") AS ?functionName)
# Filter for relevant file-manipulation APIs based on the URI's local name FILTER(REGEX(?functionName, "CreateFile|WriteFile|CopyFile|DeleteFile|MoveFile", "i"))
# 2. Find the CALL instruction targeting this function ?callInst a ontology:Instruction ; ontology:atAddress ?instructionAddress ; ontology:hasOpcode "CALL" ; ontology:hasSourceOperand ?func .
# 3. Look for instructions involving suspicious paths in either operand ?pathInst a ontology:Instruction ; (ontology:hasSourceOperand|ontology:hasDestinationOperand) ?suspiciousPath .
# 4. Filter for your specific indicators FILTER( REGEX(STR(?suspiciousPath), "System32", "i") || REGEX(STR(?suspiciousPath), "Temp", "i") || REGEX(STR(?suspiciousPath), "etc/hosts", "i") || REGEX(STR(?suspiciousPath), "vssadmin.*delete.*shadows", "i") ) }
SELECT ?functionName ?opcode ?operandName ?indicatorType WHERE { # 1. Get Function Name from the Object's URI ?func ontology:containsInstruction ?inst . BIND(REPLACE(STR(?func), "^.*[#/]", "") AS ?functionName)
# 2. Get Instruction and Opcode ?inst ontology:hasOpcode ?opcode .
# 3. Get Operand Name from either Source or Destination { ?inst ontology:hasSourceOperand ?operand . } UNION { ?inst ontology:hasDestinationOperand ?operand . }
# Extract the name/value from the operand's URI BIND(REPLACE(STR(?operand), "^.*[#/]", "") AS ?operandName)
# 4. Filter for C2 Indicators in the operand name BIND( IF(REGEX(?operandName, "^[0-9]{1,3}\\.[0-9]{1,3}\\.[0-9]{1,3}\\.[0-9]{1,3}$"), "IP Address", IF(REGEX(?operandName, "^(https?://|www\\.)", "i"), "URL", IF(REGEX(?operandName, "\\.[a-z]{2,6}$", "i"), "Domain", "Unknown"))) AS ?indicatorType )
FILTER(?indicatorType != "Unknown") } ORDER BY ?functionName
SELECT DISTINCT ?function ?loopStartAddr ?xorAddr WHERE { # 1. Scope the search to instructions within the same function ?function ontology:containsInstruction ?instr1, ?xorInstr, ?jumpInstr .
# 2. Identify the addresses ?instr1 ontology:atAddress ?loopStartAddr . ?xorInstr ontology:atAddress ?xorAddr . ?jumpInstr ontology:atAddress ?jumpAddr .
# 3. Detect a Loop: A jump instruction that points back to an earlier address ?jumpInstr ontology:hasOpcode ?jOp . FILTER(REGEX(?jOp, "^J", "i")) # Matches JMP, JNZ, JZ, etc. ?jumpInstr ontology:hasDestinationOperand ?loopStartAddr .
# Logic: The jump must go backwards (loopStartAddr < jumpAddr) FILTER(?loopStartAddr < ?jumpAddr)
# 4. Identify Bitwise/XOR logic located between the loop start and the jump ?xorInstr ontology:hasOpcode ?xorOp . FILTER(?xorOp IN ("XOR", "ROL", "ROR", "NOT", "AND"))
# Logic: XOR must be physically located within the loop boundaries FILTER(?xorAddr >= ?loopStartAddr && ?xorAddr < ?jumpAddr)
# 5. Refinement: Filter out 'XOR EAX, EAX' (common way to set register to 0) ?xorInstr ontology:hasSourceOperand ?src ; ontology:hasDestinationOperand ?dest . FILTER(?src != ?dest) }
